name: Deriv Volatility 75 (1s variant exists as 1HZ75V)
trading_hours: 24/7/365 - never closes, no sessions, no news, no gaps

engine.how_it_moves: Synthetic price generated by a cryptographically secure random number generator calibrated to a constant 75% annualized volatility. A new tick every ~2 seconds. No order book, no liquidity variation, no fundamentals.
engine.critical_implication: Because volatility is CONSTANT, sessions and time of day are IRRELEVANT. 03:00 SAST behaves identically to 15:00 SAST. Everything session-related in your forex knowledge does NOT apply here. Pure technical analysis is the only edge.

personality.summary: The most popular Deriv synthetic. Strong trending behavior with violent spike-and-retrace moves. Famous for deep wicks that hunt tight stops, then continuing the original direction. Trends on H1/H4 can run for days. The best synthetic on your list for trend day-trading; V50 is safer for scalping.
personality.typical_spread: Moderate - wider than V50, tighter than V100
behavioral_patterns: Impulse-correction rhythm is unusually clean: measured moves (impulse leg ≈ next impulse leg) work well
Spikes against the trend regularly wick 2-3x ATR before snapping back - stops must respect this
Support/resistance zones hold repeatedly because there is no news to invalidate them
Ranging periods are tight and short-lived compared to forex; breakouts follow through more honestly

key_technicals.moving_averages: M15 21 EMA holds trends beautifully; H1 50 EMA is the swing trend filter; crossovers whipsaw - use slope + structure instead
key_technicals.structure: BOS/CHoCH analysis is MORE reliable here than in forex - no news to violate structure. Trade structure religiously
key_technicals.atr: ATR(14) on the entry timeframe is your stop unit. Minimum stop = 2x ATR(M5) because of spike wicks
key_technicals.rsi: RSI(7) on M1 for scalp timing at zones; RSI divergence on M15 marks most local tops/bottoms
key_technicals.psych_levels: Reacts at large round index numbers and prior D1 swing highs/lows - mark them

scalping_plan.sessions: Any hour, 24/7 - including your full 00:00-05:00 SAST window

steps: M15 trend: price above/below 21 EMA with clean HH/HL or LL/LH
Wait for pullback to 21 EMA or the last M15 demand/supply zone
M1 trigger: RSI(7) reset (below 30 in uptrend / above 70 in downtrend) + engulfing candle
SL 2x ATR(M5) beyond the zone, TP 1:1 to 1:1.5 - take it and re-enter on the next pullback
In an established trend, wait for a sudden counter-trend spike of 2x+ ATR(M5) into an M15 zone
Enter in trend direction when M1 prints a rejection close back beyond the zone
SL beyond spike extreme, TP at pre-spike price - fast 1:1+ trades

scalping_plan.risk: 0.5-1% risk per scalp. Spikes WILL hit tight stops - size for a 2x ATR stop or do not take the trade. Never use martingale on synthetics.

steps: Enter on M15 CHoCH at H1 order block / fib 50-61.8% of the last impulse
Partial at 1R, trail behind M15 swings - V75 trend legs commonly deliver 3-5R
Exit fully on H1 CHoCH against you

after_hours_00_05_sast.conditions: Identical to any other hour - constant volatility, constant spread, full liquidity simulation.
after_hours_00_05_sast.verdict: FULLY TRADEABLE. Synthetics are the ideal instruments for your 00:00-05:00 SAST window - trade your exact daytime playbook.
playbook: Run setup_1 and setup_2 exactly as during the day
No news calendar to check - structure is never invalidated by events
Only human factor to manage is YOUR fatigue: predefine max 4-6 trades for the window

invalidations_and_traps: There is no fundamental reason for any move - never 'wait for confirmation from news', it does not exist
Tight forex-style stops (0.5x ATR) get wicked out constantly - this is the #1 V75 mistake
Counter-trend scalping against an H1 trend has terrible expectancy here - trend persistence is engineered into the vol calibration
Past RNG patterns do not repeat by time-of-day - any 'V75 moves at 3am' claim is superstition
